Marc brings a wealth of experience in navigating complex tax matters. His expertise in tax controversy is widely recognized, having assisted numerous major businesses and financial institutions across Quebec and Canada. Marc's involvement spans various sectors, both private and public, and his skilled handling of tax disputes has led to successful outcomes in cases before the Quebec court, the Tax Court of Canada, and the Court of Appeal of Quebec.
Marc has extensive experience representing a diverse clientele in various tax controversy matters, including international and domestic businesses, banks, financial institutions and government agencies. His expertise encompasses audits, relief requests and voluntary disclosures. Marc is highly active in federal and provincial income tax, international tax, Quebec sales tax, goods and services tax and harmonized sales tax matters, consistently providing comprehensive assistance in these areas.

#### Qualifications and Certifications
- Member of the Barreau de Québec
- Member of the Canadian Tax Fondation
- Member of the APFF (Association de planification fiscale et financière)
- Comité organisateur du colloque sur l’administration fiscale (APFF)
- Member of the Tax executive institute (TEI)
- The Annotated Quebec Income Tax Act, Thomson Reuters
- La Loi du Praticien - Loi sur les impôts du Québec 2022, 33e édition - Commentée | Thomson Reuters

#### Recent Speaking Engagements
- TEI conference Speaker, “Expanded CRA Audit Powers Under Bill C-32: From Coast to Coast and Beyond, ” 55th Annual Canadian Tax Conference, Tax Executives Institute (TEI), Ottawa, May 2023.
- Les pouvoirs de vérification des autorités fiscales : les grands principes, avril 2022
- APFF—Colloque, 3 - Règlement 105 (Reg. 102) : contexte de plus en plus multinational, mise en œuvre en vertu des normes actuelles, vérifications, problématiques courantes et pistes de solution (Colloque 297—Administration fiscale, 2022)

EY Law LLP is a Canadian law firm, affiliated with Ernst & Young LLP in Canada. Both EY Law LLP and Ernst & Young LLP are Ontario limited liability partnerships. For more information about the global EY organization please visit www.ey.com .
In addition to cookies that are strictly necessary to operate this website, we use the following types of cookies to improve your experience and our services: Functional cookies to enhance your experience (e.g. remember settings), Performance cookies to measure the website's performance and improve your experience, and Marketing/Targeting cookies, which are set by third parties, allow us to execute marketing campaigns, manage our relationship with you, build a profile of your interests and provide you with content or service offerings in accordance with your preferences.
You may withdraw your consent to cookies at any time once you have entered the website through a link in the cookie policy, which you can find at the bottom of each page on the website in the ‘Legal and Privacy’ section.
Review our cookie policy for more information.

### Manage Consent Preferences

#### Required/Mandatory Cookies
These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

#### Marketing/Targeting Cookies
Marketing/targeting cookies, which we use to track user activity and sessions so that we can deliver a more personalized service. Marketing cookies are set by third parties with whom we execute marketing campaigns and allow us to provide marketing relevant to you. If you identify yourself to us (e.g. filling out a form/signing up to receive email updates), EY collects information about your response to our marketing communications and your activity on this website. This information is added to your personal marketing profile and score which is used to measure campaign effectiveness and to provide content and offer services that are relevant for you.
Performance cookies, which help us measure the website’s performance and improve your experience. In using performance cookies we do not store any personal data, and only use the information collected through these cookies in aggregated and anonymized form.
